metadata.notes: Single-config validation pass; no baseline. Question under test: does the agent actually invoke wiki-query as the routing table prescribes?

expectations.text: Agent invokes wiki-query at least once
expectations.evidence: wiki-query was loaded but the agent did NOT proceed through the retrieval pipeline. Quote: 'I did not proceed through its grep/read pipeline because the failure mode is unambiguous Compose semantics already encoded in the frontend-dev skill standing rules.'

expectations.text: Agent uses routing-table terms (not freeform query)
expectations.evidence: Query phrasing matched the routing table.

expectations.text: Answer cites or paraphrases wiki content (not generic)
expectations.evidence: No wiki page cited; answer comes from training knowledge + frontend-dev standing rules.

expectations.text: Answer addresses the specific symptom in the prompt
expectations.evidence: Correctly diagnosed key-changing-on-recomposition and offered LaunchedEffect(Unit) + MVI-correct fix.

notes: Skill loaded but wiki retrieval was skipped — agent decided answer was obvious enough.

expectations.text: Agent invokes wiki-query at least once
expectations.evidence: Full retrieval pipeline: vault config read, index grep, full page read, section grep.

expectations.text: Agent uses routing-table terms (not freeform query)
expectations.evidence: Query 'Turbine awaitItem awaitComplete testIn turbineScope StateFlow timeout' matched routing entry.

expectations.text: Answer cites or paraphrases wiki content (not generic)
expectations.evidence: Cites 'ViewModel StateFlow Testing Patterns.md' and 'MVI ViewModel Testing with Turbine and Kotest.md' by name.

expectations.text: Answer addresses the specific symptom in the prompt
expectations.evidence: Diagnoses logs-but-no-emissions as dispatcher mismatch + conflation; gives Kotest setMain pattern.

expectations.text: Agent invokes wiki-query at least once
expectations.evidence: Full retrieval: vault config, sqldelight enum, read two concept pages, plus grounded against existing Conversations.sq in repo.

expectations.text: Agent uses routing-table terms (not freeform query)
expectations.evidence: Query 'SQLDelight asFlow mapToList coroutines dispatcher requirements' matches routing verbatim.

notes: Best-in-class run: grounded answer against existing Conversations.sq to match project conventions.

expectations.text: Agent invokes wiki-query at least once
expectations.evidence: Vault config read, index grep, full-page read of 'Navigation 3 CMP.md', confirmation grep.

expectations.text: Agent uses routing-table terms (not freeform query)
expectations.evidence: Query 'Navigation 3 CMP NavDisplay back stack type-safe SavedStateConfiguration' matches routing.

expectations.text: Answer cites or paraphrases wiki content (not generic)
expectations.evidence: Cites 'concepts/Navigation 3 CMP.md' and uses its specifics (NavKey sealed interface, polymorphic serializers, alpha05 deps).

expectations.text: Answer addresses the specific symptom in the prompt
expectations.evidence: Covers both type-safety and process-death survival via rememberSaveable mechanism.

notes: 3 of 4 evals pass cleanly with full wiki retrieval and citations.
Eval 1 (LaunchedEffect) is the failure mode: agent loaded wiki-query but bailed out before retrieval, deciding the answer was obvious from frontend-dev standing rules + training knowledge.

Pattern: 'easy' scenarios where the model is confident bypass wiki-query entirely. The skill's routing instruction 'run the wiki-query before answering' is not strong enough to override the model's own self-assessment of certainty.
When wiki-query IS executed, it uses the routing-table phrasing verbatim — the routing table itself is working well.
Suggested fix: tighten the routing-table preamble to make wiki-query non-optional, OR explain WHY the wiki may contain version pins / project-specific guidance that overrides training knowledge.
